title: Ranking Functions
content:
# SQL Interview Prep

## Q1: Explain why a query filtering on columns B and C cannot use a composite index defined on (A, B, C).

### Standard Answer
A composite index stores keys sorted lexicographically: first by column A, then by column B, then by column C. Because keys are sorted by A first, the index structure contains multiple disjoint blocks of B and C sorted lists. Without specifying column A in the query filter, the database engine cannot perform a binary search or index seek. It is forced to perform a full index scan or a table scan.


## Q2: How does the system handle concurrent throughput anomalies under Ranking Functions?

### Standard Answer
The system uses dynamic resource pools and queue-backoff throttles to maintain throughput boundaries, avoiding thread starvation.
